what: a16w8 page_size=16 only for bs=32,kv=1024 on the recovered hybrid baseline
why: The prior ps=16 attempt only failed when bs=64 was included; isolating bs=32 may preserve the medium-1k speedup without reintroducing the bs=64 tolerance failure, while leaving the 8k bf16 path on the proven ps=64 route.
override: The archived v152 variant was never executed because the runner was rate-limited; this retries that isolated bs=32 shape only on a clean valid baseline.

what: Route bs=256,kv=1024 to bf16_persist while keeping the isolated bs=32,kv=1024 a16w8 page_size=16 tweak
why: bs=256,kv=1024 is the heaviest remaining 1k shape, and the archived bf16-persistent route for that shape appears unexecuted. Combining it with the isolated bs=32 page-size change keeps the bs=64 1k case on the known-safe a16w8 ps=8 path.
override: The exact combination has not been run; the archived bs256 bf16-persist branch does not appear in tried.jsonl or result logs, and v156 is already in flight so this is the next queued branch.

what: Route bs=256,kv=1024 to bf16_persist on the recovered hybrid baseline
why: The heaviest 1k shape may favor the bf16 persistent path's simpler metadata and fewer pages, and the archived isolated route appears unexecuted. Keeping all other 1k shapes on their prior dispatch avoids the known bs=64 sensitivity.
override: This isolates the bs=256 1k routing change after the combined v157 branch was queued, so the effect can be attributed cleanly.

what: Route bs=256,kv=1024 to bf16_persist with page_size=128
why: The isolated bs256 1k bf16-persistent route is already in flight as v158. This follow-on tests whether the same heavy 1k shape can benefit from halving the page count again, while leaving every other shape on the recovered baseline routing.
override: The archived v151-like idea was rate-limited rather than measured, and this is the clean isolated rerun on top of the current valid baseline.

what: Combine bs=32,kv=1024 a16w8 page_size=16 with bs=256,kv=1024 bf16_persist page_size=128
why: The isolated bs32 page-size tweak and the isolated bs256 bf16 route both returned test PASS asynchronously. This combines them while still leaving bs=64 and all 8k shapes on their established routes.
override: This exact combination has not been run; it joins the two passing 1k-only changes into one candidate.

what: Use kv_granularity=32 for the bs=256,kv=1024 bf16_persist page_size=128 route while keeping the bs=32,kv=1024 a16w8 page_size=16 tweak
why: The large-page bs256 1k branch may benefit from fewer pages, but tying kv_granularity to 128 could make metadata work too coarse. This keeps the page-size reduction while restoring finer scheduling on that one heavy 1k shape.
override: This exact per-shape granularity override has not been run; prior kv_granularity results were for kv>=4096 or global settings, not this isolated 1k bs256 route.

what: Route bs=256,kv=1024 to bf16_persist with page_size=128 and kv_granularity=32
why: v159 returned test PASS for the bs256 1k large-page route. The next isolated step is to keep that route but restore finer metadata chunking, in case page_size=128 helps while kv_granularity=128 is too coarse.
override: This is a quota-deferred follow-on to the passing v159 branch; it isolates the kv_granularity change instead of mixing in the bs32 tweak.

what: Combine bs=32,kv=1024 a16w8 page_size=16 with bs=256,kv=1024 bf16_persist page_size=128 and kv_granularity=32
why: v160 and v162 each returned test PASS on their respective 1k-only routing ideas. This combines the passing medium-batch a16w8 tweak with the refined bs256 bf16-persistent route while leaving bs=64 and all 8k shapes untouched.
override: This exact composition has not been run; it adds the passing bs32 tweak on top of the isolated passing bs256+kg32 branch.

what: Resubmit the non-kg32 1k-only routing combination after reverting the broken kv_granularity override
why: v162 and v163 benchmark failures isolate kv_granularity=32 as the breaking change. v164 keeps the bs=32 a16w8 page_size=16 tweak and the bs=256 bf16_persist page_size=128 route, while removing the broken override.
override: This is a repaired resubmission after ruling out kg32 via server-side benchmark validation.
